# Error Handling Tests for MCP-Replay
#
# This test verifies that mcp-replay:
# 1. Handles malformed JSON gracefully
# 2. Continues processing valid messages even when some are invalid
# 3. Does not crash when processing invalid input

# === Malformed JSON Test ===
# Test how mcp-replay handles malformed messages (use 10x speed for faster tests)
exec mcp-replay -v -speed=10 malformed.mcp
! stdout 'panic:'
# Don't assert any specific stderr content since we're only testing that it doesn't crash

# === Mixed Valid/Invalid Messages Test ===
# Test with mixed valid and invalid messages - ensure it continues processing
exec mcp-replay -v -speed=10 mixed.mcp
! stdout 'panic:'
# The warning message goes to stderr but we don't need to check specific content

# Test Files with Embedded Timestamps (millisecond precision for timing tests)

-- malformed.mcp --
mcp-send {"this is not valid json # 1683000000.000
mcp-recv {"method":"test","params":"also broken}} # 1683000001.000
mcp-send {"valid": true, "id": "test-id"} # 1683000002.000
mcp-recv {"result": "ok", "id": "test-id"} # 1683000003.000

-- mixed.mcp --
mcp-send {"this is not valid json # 1683000000.000
mcp-send {"valid":true, "id":"request1"} # 1683000001.000
mcp-recv {"result":{"ok":true}, "id":"request1"} # 1683000001.500
mcp-recv {"method":"test","params":"also broken}} # 1683000002.000
mcp-send {"valid": true, "id": "test-id2"} # 1683000003.000
mcp-recv {"result": "ok", "id": "test-id2"} # 1683000003.500
